title: Fundraiser for the Block Island Medical Center at The National Hotel
summary: We are proud to host a fundraiser Tuesday night for the Block Island Medical Center. Come enjoy a delicious dinner and 50% of the proceeds will be donated to this amazing facility. What is better than that - eat delicious food and help raise money! Serving from 4:30 - 9 pm. Call now to make your reservation, 401-466-2901

author: Austin Crawford
title: Learn How to Shuck Oysters
summary: Shucking oysters is a skill that will never leave you. Old Island Pub is hosting Sun Farm Oysters class on shucking oysters. We will have a chain mail glove for you to use as you shuck about 8 oysters. The fee includes instruction on side shucking and hinge shucking, and you will leave with a new, maple handle, Dexter New Haven Hinge Shucker knife. We will have a special drawing for a package of 1) one side shucker knife, and 2) two cut-resistant gloves
content:
Shucking oysters is a skill that will never leave you. Old Island Pub is hosting Sun Farm Oysters class on shucking oysters. We will have a chain mail glove for you to use as you shuck about 8 oysters. The fee includes instruction on side shucking and hinge shucking, and you will leave with a new, maple handle, Dexter New Haven Hinge Shucker knife. We will have a special drawing for a package of 1) one side shucker knife, and 2) two cut-resistant gloves
We are limiting the class to 20 people so we can give individualized attention. The cost is $35/per person. The class is half full at this time.
Conquer your fear and have a beer!
Sign up at linked below:

title: Seafood Boil at Ellen’s at The Airport
summary: Time for a good ol’ Seafood Boil!
content:
Time for a good ol’ Seafood Boil!
Monday, January 26th from 5-7pm!
Please sign up by 1/20 if you’re interested. Walk ins welcome too but we would like an idea of how many boil bags to make!
$39/per boil bag which includes:
Snow Crab cluster
1/2lb of shrimp
Andouille sausage
Corn on the cob
Potatoes
Onion
Dipping Bread + Seasoned butter
Let us know by sending an email to: thecrackedmugbi@gmail.com or call us at 401-465-4413!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Spring Street Gallery Silent Art Auction & Closing Party
summary: Please join this arts community celebration and fundraiser on Sat. Oct. 11th, 5-7 pm.
content:
Please join this arts community celebration and fundraiser on Sat. Oct. 11th, 5-7 pm.

We look forward every year to seeing everyone on Columbus Day weekend, before we transition the summer gallery space to fall/winter workshops & studio space.

Thanks to everyone who supported our island artists this summer from attending our weekly receptions, to browsing the gallery, to taking a creative class.

Save this date, come visit, and put in a silent bid on a variety of art pieces by your favorite local artists. See you there.

Parking available behind Harbor Church.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Tori Jones Studio Party
summary: We're having a party tomorrow evening, August 2, at our Dodge Street shop from 5-7 PM. Join us for a glass of wine, take in evocative oil paintings by beloved artist Mary Breneman, discover New England antiques as well as delightful vintage finds and beautiful handmade goods.
content:
We're having a party tomorrow evening, August 2, at our Dodge Street shop from 5-7 PM. Join us for a glass of wine, take in evocative oil paintings by beloved artist Mary Breneman, discover New England antiques as well as delightful vintage finds and beautiful handmade goods.

We are located in the barn annex of 441 Dodge Street, behind Block Island Blue Pottery. Look for our sign at the end of the driveway.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Mary D. Ball
summary: This is a message from Marguerite Donnelly / Mary D Fund Administrator:
content:
This is a message from Marguerite Donnelly / Mary D Fund Administrator:

 

Dear Block Island Friends & Neighbors,

 

The 31st Mary D Ball is early this year on Saturday, July 26, 2025, 5-9pm under the tent at The Sullivan House.  Attendees must be 21 or older. The Fund continues to help our year-round residents ... it's our 49th year!

 

Ticket prices are $100 per person ... a reserve table for 10 is $2,500. Tickets are not necessary - a list of names will be checked at the entrance. You can send a personal or bank E-check to:  THE MARY D FUND, PO Box 323, BLOCK ISLAND 02807

 

Credit card payments will be advertised and available soon on EVENTBRITE.
address: Corn Neck Rd New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Tori Jones Studio: Summer Party at Our Shop
summary: We're having a party tomorrow evening, June 14th, at our Dodge Street shop from 5-7 PM. Join us for a glass of wine and shopping. Our store is filled with a fantastic array of handmade goods, special vintage finds, rare antiques, and the extraordinary oil paintings of celebrated artist, author, New Yorker legend, and Block Island summer resident James Stevenson.
content:
We're having a party tomorrow evening, June 14th, at our Dodge Street shop from 5-7 PM. Join us for a glass of wine and shopping. Our store is filled with a fantastic array of handmade goods, special vintage finds, rare antiques, and the extraordinary oil paintings of celebrated artist, author, New Yorker legend, and Block Island summer resident James Stevenson.


We are located in the barn annex of 441 Dodge Street, behind Block Island Blue Pottery. Look for our sign at the end of the driveway. We look forward to seeing you there!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Mystic Marine Animal Rescue Team training at BIMI
summary: Are you interested in the seals on Block Island?  Do you want to make a difference? Interested in becoming a First Responder with Mystic's Marine Animal Rescue Team? BIMI's  proud to partner with Mystic and host a  First Responder training on May 9, 2025 from 5 to 7 PM. It is our hope that we will be able to increase the size of the team on Block Island to participate both with monitoring and rescues. Please email bimi02807@gmail.com if you are interested in attending.
content:
Are you interested in the seals on Block Island?  Do you want to make a difference? Interested in becoming a First Responder with Mystic's Marine Animal Rescue Team? BIMI's  proud to partner with Mystic and host a  First Responder training on May 9, 2025 from 5 to 7 PM. It is our hope that we will be able to increase the size of the team on Block Island to participate both with monitoring and rescues. Please email bimi02807@gmail.com if you are interested in attending.

The training will be held in BIMI"s Cove Room at 216 Ocean Ave, Block Island

title: Annual Roll Call Turkey Dinner at the Harbor Church

Calling all pie makers!

October is a time of getting together, whether picking apples or just going to see old or new friends. So put on your best clothes and come to the Harbor Church Roll Call Turkey Dinner!

Bring your favorite pie to share.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Summer Symphony at Champlin's
summary: A wonderful performance of the Eastern Connecticut Symphony Orchestra under the tent at Champlin's Marina & Resort, overlooking Block Island's beautiful Great Salt Pond. With mocktails, cocktails and hors d' oeuvres starting at 5:00 and performance at 6:00pm. General admission tickets are $69.00. Please consider sponsoring this very special event.
content: A wonderful performance of the Eastern Connecticut Symphony Orchestra under the tent at Champlin's Marina & Resort, overlooking Block Island's beautiful Great Salt Pond. With mocktails, cocktails and hors d' oeuvres starting at 5:00 and performance at 6:00pm. General admission tickets are $69.00. Please consider sponsoring this very special event.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Art Class Monday at Island Bound Bookstore
summary: We still have a couple of openings for art class this Monday.
content:
We still have a couple of openings for art class this Monday.
Call Island Bound @401.466.8878 to reserve a spot.

 

These classes are appropriate for any level but are restricted to adults. Reservations are required and can be made by e-mailing island-bound@hotmail.com or calling Island Bound Bookstore at 401-466-8878. The cost is $50.00 per person and a deposit is required with your reservation. If you need to cancel, we will refund your deposit as long as we have a week's notice. Weather permitting, the classes are held on the patio in front of the bookstore. Beverages are served.
address: Water St New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Jim Wallis Book Reading and Discussion
summary: Book Reading and Discussion Group. a Forum on Democracy and Faith. Come meet and speak with NY Times Best-Selling Author and Speaker, Jim Wallis, as he promotes his newest book, "A False White Gospel: Rejecting Christian Nationalism, Reclaiming True Faith, and Refounding Democracy". Jim will be reading excerpts from his new book and hosting an open discussion forum.
content: Book Reading and Discussion Group. a Forum on Democracy and Faith. Come meet and speak with NY Times Best-Selling Author and Speaker, Jim Wallis, as he promotes his newest book, "A False White Gospel: Rejecting Christian Nationalism, Reclaiming True Faith, and Refounding Democracy". Jim will be reading excerpts from his new book and hosting an open discussion forum.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Cuban Jazz Concert Harold Lopez-Nussa: Timba a la Americana at St. Andrew's Parish
summary: Harold Lopez-Nussa is returning to Block Island. Now a Blue Note Recording Artist Harold released Timba a la Americana in August of last year. He is touring the world and will be on Block Island Monday, June 24th at the Parish Center on Spring Street. He will be joined by his brother Ruy Adrian Lopez Nussa on Drums, Luques Curtis on Bass and Gregoire Maret on harmonica. Harold sold out the Blue Note in NYC last year! He is excited to return to the Island for another concert.
content:
Harold Lopez-Nussa is returning to Block Island. Now a Blue Note Recording Artist Harold released Timba a la Americana in August of last year. He is touring the world and will be on Block Island Monday, June 24th at the Parish Center on Spring Street. He will be joined by his brother Ruy Adrian Lopez Nussa on Drums, Luques Curtis on Bass and Gregoire Maret on harmonica. Harold sold out the Blue Note in NYC last year! He is excited to return to the Island for another concert. 

 

Hosted by Will Young

title: Mary D. Ball
summary: The "Event of the Season" on Block Island.
content:
The "Event of the Season" on Block Island.
The evening begins with live music and dinner and ends with the crowd dancing into the night to the DJ.

This fundraiser supports the Mary D. Fund. The Mary D. Fund is named for Mary Donnelly - it uses the funds to assist families and individuals with bills they cannot manage in the winter months. Many senior citizens rely on the assistance with heating bills, medical bills and electric bills.
address: Corn Neck Rd New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Library Holiday Open House
summary: Visit with your friends and neighbors, sing along with the holiday music (or just listen!), and savor the delicious refreshments.
content:
Visit with your friends and neighbors, sing along with the holiday music (or just listen!), and savor the delicious refreshments.

As always we would be very grateful for contributions of appetizers, both savory and sweet, for the party. You will also have the chance to meet the new director of the Library, Robert Farwell.  Hope to see you there.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Yellow Kittens Halloween Party
summary: Don't miss our annual Halloween Party Saturday night including a costume contest. All door proceeds benefit the BI Volunteer Fire & Rescue.
content: Don't miss our annual Halloween Party Saturday night including a costume contest. All door proceeds benefit the BI Volunteer Fire & Rescue.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Roll Call Turkey Dinner
summary: Calling all pie makers!
content:
Calling all pie makers!

October is a time of getting together, whether picking apples or just going to see old or new friends. So put on your best clothes and come to the Harbor Church Roll Call Turkey Dinner!

Bring your favorite pie to share.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Art Auction Fundraiser Spring Street Gallery
summary: The auction will include a myriad of items - from prints, originals, paintings, photos, weavings and textiles, to ceramics - you are bound to find a piece you love from your favorite Block Island artists. Bid on your favorite pieces as you sip on wine, enjoy live music and snack on hors-d’oeuvres.
content:
The auction will include a myriad of items - from prints, originals, paintings, photos, weavings and textiles, to ceramics - you are bound to find a piece you love from your favorite Block Island artists. Bid on your favorite pieces as you sip on wine, enjoy live music and snack on hors-d’oeuvres.

All proceeds of the auction will benefit the Spring Street Gallery to further its non-profit mission of cultivating a creative community on Block Island.

Bidding will start at 5-6:45pm on Saturday, October 7th, 2023 at the Spring Street Gallery.

Join us at Spring Street Gallery to celebrate 41 years of appreciating our art, our people and Block Island!

title: Armenian stories
summary: There Was and There Was Not: Telling Armenian Stories: A one-woman performance by Susie Chakmakian. Spend an evening at your favorite Armenian grandma’s house as she guides you through her people’s vibrant culture and complex history, for Armenians and non-Armenians alike!
